summary: FQ-21 replaces the decompose-and-9-slice dock (rejected twice by the operator) with a one-piece full-width painted band: four native-aspect pieces sliced whole from the blueprint mockup - left health-orb cap with punched glass, right attunement-crystal cap with a luminous charge overlay, a mirror-extended clean plate tile that TILES edge-to-edge, and a fixed center block whose slot track was rebuilt uniformly. Nothing stretches, nothing is color-keyed, and all runtime coordinates come from a slicer-written geometry sidecar (dock_band_geometry.json) so hud.gd never hand-syncs mockup coordinates again. Values render on the glass per the blueprint; nav buttons are invisible click zones over the baked art; runtime selection is a gold frame overlay; command chips sit between the pedestals. Vessel sockets (vessel_socket/replace_vessel_fill) give the operator's planned liquid mechanics a smoke-proven plug-in point that any Range-derived control satisfies. Isolated-worktree smoke 319/319; gates green; band thirds visually reviewed at the operator's native 1917x1033 scale through two visual iteration loops.
scope: FQ-21 only: slicer band pieces + geometry sidecar, hud.gd band mode with modular fallback, vessel sockets, smoke evolution, verifier bound. Concurrent Codex lanes untouched and uncommitted.
next_action: Operator reviews the band in the launched instance; then FQ-22 (Diablo-1 drag-drop inventory) or the liquid-mechanics socket consumer.
